gaps.snippet: # [doc = " How a prim's operand and result *widths* relate. gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The content-addressed **prim table `Π`** (RFC-0007 §4.4; R7-Q4): resolved declarations keyed by"] # [doc = " their content hash, plus the build-time `name → hash` resolution used to form [`PrimRef`]s — the"] # [doc = " same two-map shape as [`DataRegistry`](crate::DataRegistry), so a prim's identity (`#p`) is the"] # [doc = " same on every path (the NFR-7 differential is over *one* prim set, never two)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Prims have no inter-references (unlike data, which can be mutually recursive), so building is a"] # [doc = " flat hash-and-insert — no SCC/cycle handling is needed."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Default , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct PrimTable { # [doc = " Resolved declarations, keyed by content hash."] decls : BTreeMap < ContentHash , PrimDecl > , # [doc = " Build-time (kernel) name → content hash (names are metadata — ADR-003)."] by_name : BTreeMap < String , ContentHash > , }
